methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Earth AI is a Series G infrastructure and logistics frontier-tech company founded by Roman Teslyuk that has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe is sparse: one private peer (AiDash) exists in the sector, with no public comparables available. This scarcity of narrowly-filtered peers—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier—precludes a reliable peer-median valuation method.

A single triangulation method is applicable: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a proxy baseline value of $75M, a Series G-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.25x yields an implied valuation of $94M. This method anchors to observed capital intensity and typical venture step-up patterns at this stage, though relies on the assumption that Earth AI's capital efficiency matches the underlying benchmark.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $80M to $108M (median $94M). Key uncertainties center on the limited peer set—a single Infrastructure & Logistics comparable is insufficient to validate the stage-progression result through independent cross-check. The $75M proxy baseline itself introduces estimation risk; actual implied valuation is materially dependent on whether the company's capital-to-value trajectory matches historical norms. Revenue, profitability, growth rate, and relative market position data would be required to narrow this band meaningfully.
